If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 4: you have used 283 actions; a strong score needs about 19 or fewer. You are 14.9x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I've tried many hypotheses and none have worked. Let me think about what I haven't tried yet. I need to be more systematic.
Let me list all the color assignments I've tried:
1. Red=[Y,M,O,R,p], Green=[b,N] (bottom palette, first 5 to red) - FAILED
2. Red=[Y,R,N,b,M], Green=[O,p] (top row, first 5 to red) - FAILED
3. Red=[Y,M,O,R,p], Green=[N,b] (bottom palette order, warm/cool split) - FAILED
4. Red=[O,R,p,b,N], Green=[Y,M] (bottom palette, last 5 to red) - FAILED (attempt 5)
5. Red=[N,b,M,O,p], Green=[Y,R] (top row, last 5 to red) - FAILED (attempt 6)
6. Red=[p,O,M,b,N], Green=[Y,R] (reversed top row split) - FAILED (attempt 7)
7. Red=[p,O,M,b,N], Green=[R,Y] (green reversed, red reversed) - FAILED (attempt 8)
8. Red=[N,b,M,O,p], Green=[R,Y] (green right-to-left, red left-to-right) - FAILED (attempt 9)
What I haven't tried:
- Green=[Y,M], Red=[O,R,p,b,N] - bottom palette first 2 to green, rest to red
- Green=[M,Y], Red=[O,R,p,b,N] - bottom palette first 2 reversed to green
- Green=[b,N], Red=[Y,M,O,R,p] - bottom palette last 2 to green, rest to red
- Green=[N,b], Red=[p,R,O,M,Y] - bottom palette last 2 reversed to green, rest reversed to red
- Various other orderings...
Wait, let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the key is in the CONNECTOR POSITIONS more carefully.
Red container: connectors at LEFT and RIGHT
Green container: connector at RIGHT
What if the connectors indicate the FLOW DIRECTION for each container?
- Red: LEFT -> RIGHT (fill left to right)
- Green: RIGHT -> LEFT (fill right to left, since connector is on right)
And the ORDER is determined by the container positions:
- Red is ABOVE green, so red fills first
- Then green fills
So: Red fills left-to-right first, then Green fills right-to-left.
Red: first 5 colors
Green: last 2 colors
Using TOP ROW order: Y, R, N, b, M, O, p
Red: Y, R, N, b, M (left to right)
Green: O, p (right to left means green2=O, green1=p)
Green (L->R): p, O
= Red: yellow, red, green, blue, pink
= Green: purple, orange
I haven't tried this! Let me try it.
Using BOTTOM PALETTE order: Y, M, O, R, p, b, N
Red: Y, M, O, R, p (left to right)
Green: b, N (right to left means green2=b, green1=N)
Green (L->R): N, b
= Red: yellow, pink, orange, red, purple
= Green: green, blue
I haven't tried this either! Let me try the top row version first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Hypothesis: Red fills first (left-to-right), Green fills second (right-to-left)
# Using TOP ROW order: Y, R, N, b, M, O, p
# Red: Y, R, N, b, M (left to right)
# Green: O, p (right to left: green2=O, green1=p)
# Result: Red=[Y,R,N,b,M], Green=[p,O]

bottom = {
    'Y': (58, 10),  # yellow
    'M': (58, 17),  # pink
    'O': (58, 24),  # orange
    'R': (58, 31),  # red
    'p': (58, 38),  # purple
    'b': (58, 45),  # blue
    'N': (58, 52),  # green
}

red_slots = [(22, 19), (22, 25), (22, 31), (22, 37), (22, 43)]
green_slots = [(36, 31), (36, 37)]

# Fill red left-to-right: Y, R, N, b, M
# Fill green right-to-left: O, p (green2=O, green1=p)
plan = [
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['Y'][0], 'col': bottom['Y'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': red_slots[0][0], 'col': red_slots[0][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['R'][0], 'col': bottom['R'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': red_slots[1][0], 'col': red_slots[1][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['N'][0], 'col': bottom['N'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': red_slots[2][0], 'col': red_slots[2][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['b'][0], 'col': bottom['b'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': red_slots[3][0], 'col': red_slots[3][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['M'][0], 'col': bottom['M'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': red_slots[4][0], 'col': red_slots[4][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['O'][0], 'col': bottom['O'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': green_slots[1][0], 'col': green_slots[1][1]},  # green2=O
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': bottom['p'][0], 'col': bottom['p'][1]},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': green_slots[0][0], 'col': green_slots[0][1]},  # green1=p
    {'action': 'SPACE'},
]

action(plan)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
